description:
Perform a causal inference analysis on the LaLonde (1986) job training dataset to estimate the effect of the National Supported Work (NSW) program on earnings (re78). Your goal is to correct for selection bias using Propensity Score Matching (PSM) with the 'lalonde' dataset.

The analysis must compare a naive unadjusted difference in mean earnings against two matching methods: Nearest Neighbor (1:1) and Full Matching. Evaluate covariate balance using standardized mean differences (SMD) and estimate the Average Treatment Effect on the Treated (ATT) for the naive comparison and both matching methods.

Save the following deliverables to /home/ga/RProjects/output/:
1. 'lalonde_balance_table.csv': Must contain covariate names and SMD values for Unmatched and Matched samples.
2. 'lalonde_treatment_effects.csv': Must contain columns for 'Method' (e.g., Naive, NN, Full) and 'Estimate' (the dollar value of the effect).
3. 'lalonde_love_plot.png': A plot visualizing the balance improvement.
4. 'lalonde_analysis.R': Your complete R script.
